name: Firdevs
classification_notes: Firdevs shows elegant, hand-drawn letterform origins with organic stroke contrast and warmth consistent with humanist serif traditions, suited to both display and text use.
summary: A single-weight serif drawn entirely by hand in a Turkish prison cell by award-winning visual journalist Fevzi Yazıcı, then digitised by Dyana Weissman.
description: Conceived by Yazıcı during his imprisonment at Silivri with only pencil and paper, the typeface realises a long-held ambition to design letterforms from scratch. Named after his wife, it combines elegance with legibility across display and text sizes, and has been used in exhibition posters and catalogues. All profits from the font go to the Yazıcı family.
foundry_blurb: Kerns & Cairns describe Firdevs as born from extraordinary circumstances: drawn entirely by hand in a prison cell by Fevzi Yazıcı, a visual journalist with over 100 industry awards for his work at Istanbul's Zaman newspaper, and subsequently digitised by type designer Dyana Weissman. The typeface, named after Yazıcı's wife, has been used in exhibitions and catalogues, and all profits support the Yazıcı family.

name: Glissade
classification_notes: Glissade draws directly from the lettering tradition of WPA National Parks posters, which sit squarely in the Art Deco display vernacular. The low-contrast variant (Monoline) distinguishes it further, but the primary design intent is decorative and display-oriented.
summary: A WPA National Parks poster-inspired display family with contrast and monoline variants across standard, condensed and compressed widths.
description: Taking its cues from the lettering of WPA National Parks posters, Glissade offers a high-contrast display cut alongside a low-contrast Monoline version across seven weights from Light to Black. The family spans three widths — standard, Condensed and Compressed — with variable fonts available throughout, giving designers considerable flexibility for poster and branding work. Swashy R and Q tail alternates add period-appropriate character to the set.
foundry_blurb: Kerns & Cairns describe Glissade as inspired by WPA National Parks posters, with the Monoline variant born from curiosity about how those designs would look with reduced contrast. The collection is noted as still in progress, with the option to contact the foundry about completing it or customising it for a specific brand. Illustrations in the promotional material are by Laura Serra.

name: Apotek
classification_notes: A sans-serif with a commanding, characterful personality drawn from antique Norwegian apothecary label lettering; the sturdy uppercase-driven origins and range of widths place it firmly in the grotesque tradition with display leanings.
summary: A large grotesque family rooted in the lettering of antique Norwegian medicine bottle labels, expanded across seven widths and seven weights with full italic coverage and variable font support.
description: Drawn from the commanding, quirk-filled uppercase lettering found on old Norwegian apothecary labels, Apotek has been developed into a vast system spanning Compressed through Extended widths, ExtraLight through Black weights, and matching italics throughout. The family is available as static fonts and as variable fonts with continuous axes for both width and weight. OpenType features include Rounded and Bowed alternates that bring the source material's idiosyncratic warmth closer to the surface.
foundry_blurb: Kerns & Cairns describe Apotek as inspired by the labels on antique Norwegian medicine bottles, whose letters caught the designer's eye with their commanding personality and delightful quirks. Starting from mainly uppercase sources in a single style, the idea was stretched into a larger range of weights and widths, seeking a balance between the charming source material and a broadly useful design. Illustrations for the collection are by Laura Serra, and the family is also available through Adobe Fonts.
